palette_hint: Off-white background #F5F0E8, near-black text #1A1612, with burnt sienna accent #C4502A and pale mineral blue #A8C4CC for pull-quotes and figure labels.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif at heavy weight with wide letter-spacing for geological gravitas; body in system-ui at 400 weight, 1.75 line-height for long-form readability; captions in monospace for data/specimen feel.
description: A long-form multimedia essay exploring the geology, ecology, and human history of the Bonneville Salt Flats, structured as five thematic chapters each with its own visual grammar.
constraints: Each page uses exactly three distinct layout regions: a full-bleed chapter header (100vw, min-height 38vh) with large centered type over a CSS-pattern background, a main essay column (max-width 720px, margin auto, padding 0 24px), and a sidebar annotation zone (280px wide, position sticky at top 48px) that appears beside the essay column via CSS grid with columns '1fr 280px' gap 48px at viewport ≥ 1100px.
Pull-quotes must be set in Georgia italic at 1.6rem, color #C4502A, left-bordered 4px solid #C4502A, padding-left 24px, breaking the 720px essay column to span into the sidebar lane.
Figure captions and specimen labels use monospace font-size 0.75rem, color #A8C4CC, uppercase letter-spacing 0.12em, always placed below their associated bordered box (2px solid #A8C4CC).

Section rhythm breaks between essay subsections use a 64px top margin, a 1px horizontal rule in #C4502A at 40px width centered, and 32px bottom margin before the next heading — no other dividers allowed.

CSS geometric background patterns on chapter headers (repeating-linear-gradient only, no external images) must differ per page: threshold uses diagonal 45deg lines, stratigraphy uses horizontal strata bands, halophytes uses a fine dot grid, human-marks uses a cross-hatch, and dissolution uses concentric rectangle outlines via layered gradients.

page_specs.threshold: The opening chapter establishes the essay's premise with a header reading 'A Crust of Crystallized Time' over a diagonal-stripe CSS pattern in pale sienna on off-white. The 720px essay column opens with two dense paragraphs on the Pleistocene Lake Bonneville receding 13,000 years ago, followed by a pull-quote: 'To walk on salt is to walk on an evaporated inland sea.' The sticky sidebar holds a hand-annotated-style text box listing key statistics — 30,000 acres, 12% sodium chloride, elevation 4,218 ft — in monospace against a #A8C4CC-bordered rectangle.
page_specs.stratigraphy: This chapter uses the strata-band header pattern to mirror the subject: the layered sediment record beneath the flats. The essay column alternates between prose paragraphs and narrow 'core sample' figures — vertical CSS rectangles 60px wide and 200px tall with banded background colors representing clay, salt, and gravel layers, each with a monospace caption. The sidebar displays a hand-lettered-style depth index from 0m to 9m with tick marks built entirely from CSS borders and spans.
page_specs.halophytes: Focused on the salt-tolerant plant communities at the flats' margins — pickleweed, iodine bush, and greasewood — this page's dot-grid header pattern evokes cellular biology. The essay is broken into three named subsections ('Pioneers,' 'Specialists,' 'Ghosts') each introduced by a bold Georgia all-caps label. Each subsection contains a pull-quote in sienna and a sidebar annotation describing one species' adaptation strategy in 40–60 words, framed in the blue-bordered monospace style.
page_specs.human-marks: Covering land speed record attempts from 1914 to Thrust SSC in 1997, this chapter's cross-hatch header evokes graph paper and engineering diagrams. The essay column includes a horizontal timeline built with CSS flexbox — seven labeled events spaced evenly across the full 720px column width, each marked with a 10px sienna square and a two-line monospace date/name label below. The sidebar holds a glossary of racing terminology (measured mile, black-smoke flag, course preparation) in tight monospace rows.
page_specs.dissolution: The closing chapter addresses accelerating salt crust deterioration from groundwater pumping and tourism. The concentric-rectangle header pattern — built from layered repeating-linear-gradients forming nested boxes — visualizes shrinkage. The essay ends with a three-column closing statement grid (each column max-width 220px, gap 24px, centered within the 720px lane) offering three interpretive framings: geological, ecological, and political. The sidebar is empty and labeled only with the monospace line 'no annotation required' in #A8C4CC, as a deliberate formal choice.
